summary: McKenzie highlights 'Don't Wreck My PC' as the best app name among participants in a development sprint. He praises the name for its brandability and ability to serve as both a product name and unique selling proposition. He also compliments the logo's bright, colorful design and recommends skinning the app to match. He shares that making his own app look more 'Fisher Pricey' improved conversions by about 50%.
key_points: A program name that doubles as a USP is extremely valuable for marketing
Keywords in program names help with SEO through anchor text, but brandability matters more for sales
Bright, colorful B2C design that looks approachable (Fisher Price-style) can dramatically improve conversions
Visual consistency between logo and app UI helps drive conversions
money_quotes: Your program name is also your USP. Sweet deal.
Bright and colorful does great things in B2C.
Oh crikey does that help conversions (by about 50% when I made my app look more Fisher Pricey).
key_insight: The best product names simultaneously serve as a unique selling proposition, and matching your app's visual design to a bright, approachable aesthetic can dramatically improve conversion rates in B2C markets.
